description:
The nss-tools package can be installed with the following command: 
$ sudo dnf install nss-tools
rationale:
Network Security Services (NSS) is a set of libraries designed to
support cross-platform development of security-enabled client and
server applications. Install the <tt>nss-tools</tt> package
to install command-line tools to manipulate the NSS certificate
and key database.
ocil_clause: the nss-tools package is not installed
ocil:
Run the following command to determine if the nss-tools package is installed: 
$ rpm -q nss-tools
fixtext:
To install the Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 package nss-tools run the following command:

$ sudo dnf install nss-tools
srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must have the nss-tools package installed.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must have the nss-tools package installed.
stig.vuldiscussion: Network Security Services (NSS) is a set of libraries designed to support cross-platform development of security-enabled client and server applications. Install the "nss-tools" package to install command-line tools to manipulate the NSS certificate and key database.
stig.checktext:
Verify that Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 has the nss-tools package installed with the following command:

$ dnf list --installed nss-tools

Example output:

nss-tools.x86_64          3.71.0-7.el9

If the "nss-tools" package is not installed, this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
The nss-tools package can be installed with the following command:

$ sudo dnf install nss-tools

title: Ensure nss-tools is installed
